business_description: Restricts a party from competing in certain markets, geographies, or sectors.
Creates exclusive dealing obligations that may limit business flexibility.
Restricts solicitation of the counterparty’s customers or partners.
Restricts hiring or solicitation of the counterparty’s employees.
Allows termination without cause after notice or waiting period.
Creates rights or obligations triggered by ownership or control changes.
Restricts transfer or assignment of contractual rights.
Requires sharing revenue or profit with the counterparty.
Restricts the ability to change prices.
Requires minimum purchase, order, usage, or payment commitments.
Creates restrictions or penalties based on usage or volume thresholds.
Transfers ownership of created intellectual property to another party.
Creates shared ownership of intellectual property.
Grants rights to use intellectual property, technology, content, or services.
Grants broad or unlimited usage rights.
Creates license rights that may survive indefinitely or cannot be revoked.
Creates obligations that continue after contract termination.
Allows one party to inspect records, books, systems, or compliance evidence.
Indicates whether a party may face liability without a contractual cap.
Indicates whether the contract limits the maximum liability exposure.
Defines preset damages or fees for breach or termination.
Specifies how long warranties remain valid.
Indicates whether a party must maintain insurance coverage.
